lede: A look at May 5 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Søren Kierkegaard and Karl Marx, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: World Health Organization
events.desc: The World Health Organization declares the end of the COVID-19 pandemic as a global health emergency.

events.title: Kenya Airways Flight 507
events.desc: Kenya Airways Flight 507 crashes after takeoff from Douala International Airport in Douala, Cameroon, killing all 114 aboard, making it the deadliest aircraft disaster in Cameroon.

events.title: Caning of Michael Fay
events.desc: American teenager Michael P. Fay is caned in Singapore for theft and vandalism.

events.title: 1991 Washington, D.C., riot
events.desc: A riot breaks out in the Mt. Pleasant section of Washington, D.C. after police shoot a Salvadoran man.

events.title: Project Mercury
events.desc: Project Mercury: Alan Shepard becomes the first American to travel into outer space, on a sub-orbital flight.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Norwegian campaign: Norwegian squads in Hegra Fortress and Vinjesvingen capitulate to German forces after all other Norwegian forces in southern Norway had laid down their arms.

events.title: Pitcher
events.desc: Pitching against the Philadelphia Athletics at the Huntington Avenue Grounds, Cy Young of the Boston Americans throws the first perfect game in the modern era of baseball.

events.title: Carnegie Hall
events.desc: The Music Hall in New York City (later known as Carnegie Hall) has its grand opening and first public performance, with Tchaikovsky as the guest conductor.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: The Battle of the Wilderness begins in Spotsylvania County.

events.title: Cromwell's Act of Grace
events.desc: Cromwell's Act of Grace, aimed at reconciliation with the Scots, proclaimed in Edinburgh.

births.name: Henryk Sienkiewicz
births.line: Polish journalist and author, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: Ludwig Erhard, German economist and politician, Chancellor of Germany
Peter Gustav Lejeune Dirichlet, German mathematician and academic
Frederick Augustus I of Saxony, Ruler of Saxony from 1763 to 1827
Casimir II the Just, Polish son of Bolesław III Wrymouth

observances.text: Christian feast day: Blessed Edmund Ignatius Rice
Christian feast day: Frederick the Wise (Lutheran Church–Missouri Synod)
Christian feast day: Blessed Grzegorz Bolesław Frąckowiak
